title: Smart Home Energy Reduction
description: What practical strategies can significantly lower energy consumption in AI‑powered smart homes?
narrative: The user seeks evidence-based solutions focused on measurable efficiency gains, affordability, and compatibility with existing IoT ecosystems, prioritizing methods that can be implemented in current smart‑home infrastructures.

title: Solar Feasibility Metrics
description: Which metrics are most commonly used to evaluate the feasibility of solar photovoltaic installations on commercial rooftops?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed articles detailing solar feasibility assessment tools, including financial analysis, energy yield estimation, resource assessment, and regulatory compliance, while excluding general consumer guides and marketing materials.

title: Monarch Health
description: What were the documented health issues of European monarchs in the late 19th century, and how did those problems influence their reigns?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed exploration of monarchical health conditions—including medical records, contemporary accounts, and scholarly analyses—to understand how ailments such as rheumatic fever, anemia, or neurological disorders impacted decision‑making, governance style, and succession in courts like that of Russia, Britain, and other key European states during the 1800s.

title: Coastal Adaptation Techniques
description: What evidence-based techniques are most effective for adapting coastal cities to sea‑level rise and increased storm surges?
narrative: Seeking peer‑reviewed studies, policy reports, and case studies that evaluate long‑term outcomes of adaptation interventions (e.g., levee reinforcement, managed retreat, nature‑based solutions) in cities with similar socioeconomic profiles and environmental challenges. Relevance requires concrete effectiveness data rather than theoretical proposals.

title: Green Building Certifications
description: Please list the major green building certification schemes used worldwide and explain their key criteria.
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on environmentally friendly building certification programs, wants to compare schemes such as LEED, BREEAM, WELL, etc., and requires details on their evaluation criteria, scoring systems, and global adoption. Relevant results should include authoritative sources, documentation, and comparison analyses but should exclude general marketing or unrelated industry press.

title: Adaptive Learning Systems
description: What are the most effective adaptive learning technologies used in higher education for personalized student outcomes?
narrative: The user is seeking evidence on adaptive learning tools that tailor instructional content, pacing, and assessment to individual student needs. Relevance is judged by peer‑reviewed studies, institutional case reports, and analytical frameworks that demonstrate measurable improvements in engagement and academic performance.

title: Blockchain Scalability Solutions
description: How can blockchain networks achieve high throughput without sacrificing security or decentralization?
narrative: The user seeks comparative analyses of protocol-level and layer‑2 techniques, including optimistic roll‑ups, sharding, and state‑channels, focusing on trade‑offs in latency, cost, and consensus robustness. Relevance requires recent academic or industry studies, metrics, and real‑world implementations, not general hype.

title: Middle School STEM Curiosity
description: How can teachers actively foster scientific curiosity in middle school STEM classrooms?
narrative: The user seeks concrete, evidence‑based strategies that have been tested in elementary or middle school contexts, focusing on curricular activities, inquiry‑based lessons, assessment techniques, and interdisciplinary linkages. Relevance is judged by the presence of empirical data, examples of successful implementation, and clear guidance for classroom practice.

title: Reducing Urban Food Waste
description: What evidence-based strategies effectively lower household food waste in large cities?
narrative: The user is a city planner seeking actionable, research-backed measures—such as policy interventions, consumer education programs, or technological tools—whose impact on food waste reduction can be quantified in urban contexts. Relevance requires studies or reports that present measurable outcomes in metropolitan settings, rather than theoretical or small-scale examples.

title: Open-Source HPC ML
description: What are the latest open-source frameworks for distributed machine learning suitable for high-performance computing (HPC) environments?
narrative: The user wants to find current open-source projects that enable distributed training of machine learning models on HPC clusters. Relevance requires recent releases (within the last 2 years), evidence of scalability, integration with popular libraries such as PyTorch or TensorFlow and MPI, as well as documentation and community activity. Articles should mention real HPC deployments and provide performance benchmarks or case studies.

title: Nomad Visas
description: Which countries offer digital nomad visas and what are their eligibility requirements?
narrative: The user is a remote worker interested in traveling while working and wants concise, reliable information about countries that provide digital nomad visas, including eligibility criteria, visa duration, fees, and application steps.

title: Eco Packaging Solutions
description: Which recent innovations in biodegradable packaging have been adopted by major food manufacturers in 2024?
narrative: The user seeks specific information on recent biodegradable packaging technologies adopted by large food companies in 2024, focusing on case studies, technology details, and adoption timelines. Documents describing lab research, pilot projects, or conceptual designs are irrelevant unless tied to 2024 adoption by major brands. Exclude generic discussions about environmental packaging without concrete examples from 2024.

title: Low-Frequency Detection
description: What are the latest portable low-frequency radar detection systems for use in civilian security contexts?
narrative: The user is a security professional seeking state‑of‑the‑art sensor solutions capable of detecting stealth or low‑altitude threats near critical infrastructure. Relevant documents should detail sensor architecture, signal processing techniques, deployment scenarios, and empirical performance data. Documents that focus solely on theoretical radar physics without practical validation or field test results are considered irrelevant.

title: Sustainable Urban Lifestyles
description: What are the most effective strategies for reducing personal carbon footprints in urban settings, according to recent peer‑reviewed research?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date, evidence‑based approaches to minimizing environmental impact while living in a city, prioritizing practical, measurable actions and studies published within the past decade, and is less interested in theoretical or historical discussions.

title: Virtual Reality in Stroke Rehab
description: What is the effectiveness of virtual reality interventions for improving motor function in adult stroke survivors?
narrative: The user is a rehabilitation researcher seeking up-to-date, peer-reviewed evidence that evaluates virtual reality therapy protocols, focusing on studies that report quantitative outcomes for upper-limb motor recovery in adults post-stroke, excluding pediatric populations and animal studies.

title: Vehicle Battery Supply Chains
description: What are the main challenges facing electric vehicle battery supply chains worldwide?
narrative: The user seeks insight into the primary obstacles—such as resource scarcity, geopolitical tensions, and manufacturing bottlenecks—that affect the availability and sustainability of EV batteries. Relevant documents should analyze supply chain dynamics, logistics, risk mitigation, and policy considerations, while documents unrelated to battery supply chains or that focus on unrelated technical details should be considered irrelevant.

title: Climate-Resilient Materials
description: What building materials are labeled as climate‑resilient for hurricane‑prone coastal regions?
narrative: The user is an architect planning residential projects in U.S. coastal zones that are repeatedly hit by hurricanes. They need detailed information on materials that meet or exceed FEMA and NIST hurricane‑resilience standards, including certification status, cost per unit, installation requirements, and environmental impact. Documents that discuss only general construction materials, unrelated climate resilience measures, or non‑hurricane‑related regions should be considered irrelevant.

title: Plastic-Free Hospitality
description: What alternative sustainable packaging materials can hotels use to replace single‑use plastics in their rooms and dining services?
narrative: The user is a hotel procurement manager seeking eco‑friendly, cost‑effective packaging solutions that reduce environmental impact while maintaining guest comfort; relevant documents must discuss materials, life‑cycle analysis, implementation strategies, and real‑world case studies.

title: Crypto Regulatory Landscape
description: What are the latest regulatory frameworks governing cryptocurrencies in the European Union?
narrative: The user is a regulatory affairs specialist for a fintech startup seeking detailed, authoritative, and actionable information on EU-level directives, national statutes, and EU court rulings that impact cryptocurrency exchanges, wallet providers, and token issuances. Relevance is defined by documents that include analysis of legal texts, implementation guidelines, compliance timelines, real-world case studies, and documented enforcement actions.

title: Urban Farming Comparison
description: What are the main differences in yield, cost, and environmental impact between hydroponic and soil-based urban farming methods?
narrative: The user wants an evaluative comparison that includes quantitative metrics (yield per square meter), cost analysis (setup and operational), and sustainability aspects (water use, energy, and carbon footprint), while excluding anecdotal or purely theoretical discussions.

title: Mobile Payment Security
description: What are the main security threats to mobile payment systems?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of vulnerabilities affecting mobile payment platforms, focusing on third‑party SDK integration, cross‑device attacks, and user credential management. Relevant documents should discuss risk categories, preventive measures, and real‑world incident examples.

title: Startup Funding Landscape
description: What are the most effective funding strategies for early-stage technology startups in emerging markets?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed overview of funding mechanisms—angel investors, venture capital, crowdfunding, and government grants—emphasizing success factors, typical timelines, and real‑world examples from Southeast Asia. Papers or reports that discuss generic investment theory or reference unrelated business model frameworks should be excluded. Relevant information must cite specific startups, provide data on funding rounds, and explain how each strategy aligns with market dynamics in emerging economies.

title: Mobile Banking Privacy
description: How can mobile banking applications best safeguard user data privacy?
narrative: The user is a product manager at a fintech company who wants actionable guidance on implementing privacy features that comply with global regulations for mobile banking apps. Relevance should be judged by whether the document discusses specific privacy techniques (e.g., end‑to‑end encryption, secure key management, local data storage controls, biometric authentication), regulatory frameworks (GDPR, CCPA, PCI‑DSS, PSD2), privacy‑by‑design principles, and real‑world mobile banking case studies. Documents that cover general data privacy, non‑mobile banking contexts, or abstract policy discussions without concrete implementation details should be considered less relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the most recent international policies promoting renewable energy and their effectiveness?
narrative: A policy analyst seeks a comparative review of international renewable energy agreements, looking for documents published after 2018 that discuss implementation strategies, measurable outcomes, and stakeholder impacts. Relevant documents must provide evaluative evidence of policy success or shortcomings; purely technical reports or pre‑2018 materials are excluded.

title: Remote Work Impact
description: What are the effects of remote work arrangements on employee productivity and well‑being?
narrative: The user seeks research that evaluates how working from home influences worker output, engagement, and health, and is particularly interested in empirical studies, surveys, and reports that provide quantitative findings or credible policy analyses.

title: AI Diagnostics
description: How can AI models be integrated into routine healthcare diagnostics to improve accuracy and efficiency?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based, practical guidance on deploying machine‑learning tools in clinical practice. Relevant documents should provide comparative studies, case reports, or implementation frameworks that address regulatory compliance, patient data privacy, workflow integration, and measurable outcomes related to diagnostic performance. The focus should be on approaches that have been successfully implemented in real‑world healthcare settings rather than purely theoretical or experimental research.

title: Global Climate Policy
description: What are the most effective international agreements for reducing carbon emissions?
narrative: The user is researching how international environmental agreements contribute to carbon emission reductions. They are interested in policies that are broadly adopted, have measurable impact metrics, and include enforcement mechanisms. Relevance is determined by the presence of evidence-based assessments, comparative analyses of agreement performance, and discussion of implementation barriers.

title: Specialized Cardiovascular Therapy
description: What are the latest evidence-based interventions for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy in patients over 60?
narrative: The user is a cardiologist seeking up-to-date, peer-reviewed clinical trials and systematic reviews that provide outcome data for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy management in the elderly, focusing on pharmacologic and interventional options, patient demographics, and follow-up periods.

title: Arctic Ice Trends
description: What are the recent trends in Arctic sea ice extent and how are they projected to change in the next decade?
narrative: The user is a climate scientist looking for peer‑reviewed studies that quantify recent sea‑ice loss, incorporate satellite observations and climate model projections, and forecast future trends over the next ten years, emphasizing data from the last two decades.

title: Urban Food Waste
description: What are the most effective strategies for reducing food waste in large city restaurants?
narrative: The user is an operations manager at a chain of urban restaurants looking for evidence-based interventions that reduce waste while preserving quality. Relevant documents should contain case studies, statistical analyses, cost-benefit evaluations, and best-practice guidelines from peer-reviewed or industry-recognized sources, and should focus specifically on high-volume, urban kitchen environments.

title: Remote Work Commute
description: How has the rise of remote work affected daily commuting patterns and traffic congestion in major U.S. urban centers?
narrative: The user seeks recent academic studies, government reports, and reputable news analyses that quantify changes in commute times, modal share shifts, and congestion levels since the widespread adoption of remote work post‑2020. Relevance is determined by the inclusion of empirical data or systematic reviews, mention of specific urban regions (e.g., New York, San Francisco, Chicago), and focus on measurable transportation impacts rather than anecdotal or purely qualitative observations.

title: Remote Work Adoption
description: What factors influence small businesses' decision to adopt remote work policies post‑pandemic?
narrative: The user wants a detailed, evidence‑based overview of the drivers, incentives, and barriers that shape remote‑work adoption among small enterprises, including case studies, economic analyses, and policy implications.

title: EU Digital Trade pacts
description: Which bilateral trade agreements between the European Union and other countries include explicit provisions on digital services and data protection?
narrative: The user seeks to identify current EU trade agreements that specifically address digital services and data protection. Relevance criteria: the agreement must be signed or in force, contain clauses related to digital services and data protection, and reference both. Exclude general trade discussions, agreements lacking digital provisions, or agreements that do not involve the EU.

title: Autonomous Vehicle Adoption
description: What factors influence consumer acceptance of self‑driving cars in densely populated cities?
narrative: The user seeks a multifaceted analysis of the socio‑economic, psychological, and infrastructural determinants that shape the likelihood of widespread autonomous vehicle deployment in metropolitan areas.  Relevance requires documents that present empirical studies, policy analyses, case reports, or surveys examining variables such as trust, cost, safety records, legal frameworks, and infrastructure readiness, while addressing diverse demographic groups.  General overviews of autonomous technology without a specific focus on adoption factors are not relevant.

title: Sustainable Urban Farming
description: Which small-scale urban farming initiatives in major U.S. cities effectively combine revenue generation with community education programs?
narrative: A researcher seeks case studies demonstrating profitability and educational impact of urban farms to develop best practices for community-based agriculture projects. Relevance is judged by the presence of measurable financial sustainability, evidence of community outreach, and practical design guidelines suitable for replication.

title: Solar-Powered Urban Cars
description: What are the latest developments in solar-powered electric vehicles for city use?
narrative: The user wants recent, practical information on commercially available or prototype solar‑electric vehicles designed for urban commuting. Relevance is judged by how well a source addresses performance metrics, cost, range, and deployment timelines, while sources that discuss purely academic studies or speculative concepts are considered non‑relevant.

title: AI Interpretability Health
description: What are the current methods for explaining deep learning decisions in medical diagnosis and how are they validated?
narrative: The user is a medical researcher looking for a concise overview of deep‑learning interpretability techniques that have been applied to clinical diagnosis tasks. They want a comparison of methods such as saliency maps, SHAP, LIME, and counterfactual explanations, along with details on how each is validated—e.g., correlation with radiologist annotations, clinical trial outcomes, or quantitative metrics. Relevance criteria require peer‑reviewed studies published from 2015 onward that demonstrate these techniques in real‑world healthcare settings, include results from validation experiments, and discuss practical implementation considerations for clinicians.

title: Medication Overuse Headache Prevention
description: What strategies can effectively prevent medication overuse headaches in adults using frequent acute headache medication?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based recommendations that can help adults avoid developing medication overuse headaches. They want up‑to‑date, peer‑reviewed information on behavioral interventions, alternative therapies, and drug‑cessation protocols, including monitoring guidelines and tapering schedules that have been shown to reduce headache frequency and severity while minimizing rebound risk.

title: Remote Learning Effectiveness
description: How effective has remote learning been for high‑school students during the COVID‑19 pandemic?
narrative: The user wants a synthesis of peer‑reviewed studies and government reports that compare academic achievement, student engagement, and equity impacts between remote and in‑person instruction for high‑school students, with a focus on data collected from 2020 to 2022.

title: Urban Transit Sustainability
description: How can urban public transit systems be redesigned to minimize environmental impact while maintaining accessibility?
narrative: The user seeks academic articles, case studies, and empirical data that discuss strategies and policy measures for reducing carbon emissions in urban transit, emphasizing interventions that preserve or enhance mobility equity and service quality. Relevance is judged by evidence-based analysis, multi-modal coordination, and implementation examples.

title: Mixed Reality Learning
description: How can educators integrate mixed reality into K‑12 curricula to enhance student engagement and learning outcomes?
narrative: The user is a K‑12 teacher or curriculum designer seeking evidence‑based guidance on incorporating VR and AR technologies. Relevance must include scholarly studies, case reports, pedagogical frameworks, pilot project summaries, and practical tool recommendations that evaluate engagement, retention, and learning outcomes. Exclude generic marketing material, unrelated technical tutorials, and testimonials without research backing.

title: Women in STEM Careers
description: What barriers do women face in entering STEM careers, and what initiatives effectively support them?
narrative: The user is an academic researcher studying gender diversity in higher education and industry. They require scholarly, policy‑focused, and case‑study documents that discuss societal, institutional, and individual obstacles, evidence of successful interventions, measurable outcomes, and recommended best practices. Relevance is judged by depth of analysis, recent empirical data, and explicit linkage between identified barriers and implemented support programs.

title: Urban Agriculture Policy
description: What are the effective policies that promote sustainable urban agriculture in major cities worldwide?
narrative: The user seeks specific legislative or regulatory measures that demonstrably increased urban farming adoption, focusing on cities with populations over 1 million and excluding broad sustainability initiatives not directly linked to farm production.

title: Climate Adaptation Planning
description: What are effective strategies for communities to adapt to climate change impacts?
narrative: The user is an urban planner or researcher looking for actionable, evidence-based measures to mitigate climate risks in local settings. Relevant documents should provide detailed adaptation interventions, cost-benefit analyses, policy frameworks, success case studies, and implementation challenges, rather than generic or purely theoretical discussions.

title: Remote Learning Efficacy
description: What factors most influence the academic performance of graduate students engaged in fully online courses during the COVID-19 pandemic?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence-based determinants of success in remote graduate education. Relevant documents should report empirical studies, meta-analyses, or systematic reviews that identify measurable outcomes and contextual factors—such as instructional design, learner engagement, technology accessibility, and support services—affecting student performance.

title: Remote Work Tools
description: What platforms and tools are most effective for maintaining productivity and collaboration among software engineers working remotely?
narrative: The user is a manager of a distributed software engineering team seeking to benchmark tools that enhance task tracking, code review, and communication. Relevant documents must contain evidence-based comparisons between platforms (e.g., Jira, GitHub, Slack, Zoom), discuss implementation challenges, user experience, and cost analysis. Documents that mention generic non-digital productivity methods or unrelated tool categories should be considered less relevant.

title: Urban Agriculture Sustainability
description: What are the most effective practices for reducing the environmental footprint of urban farms while maintaining high yields?
narrative: The user is researching sustainable methods in dense city settings, looking for evidence‑based strategies that balance ecological impact and productivity. Relevant documents should discuss agroecological techniques, waste recycling, energy optimization, and case studies of successful urban farms. Documents that merely discuss general farming or rural agriculture without a clear urban context are less relevant.

title: Quantum Computing
description: What are the current real-world applications of quantum computing in industry?
narrative: The user seeks concrete examples of how quantum computers are being deployed today, beyond theoretical research. Relevant documents should include recent case studies, industry reports, or product releases in sectors such as pharmaceuticals, finance, logistics, or materials science. Documents that only discuss theoretical algorithms or early-stage research without real-world deployment should be considered non-relevant.

title: Sustainable Food Systems
description: What policies and practices have been most effective in promoting sustainable food systems in low‑income urban areas?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based information on urban agriculture, food‑justice initiatives, and policy interventions that improve food security, environmental sustainability, and community resilience in economically disadvantaged inner‑city neighborhoods. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed studies, grey literature from NGOs, city‑government reports, and case‑study analyses that quantify socioeconomic, environmental, and health outcomes of implemented programs.

title: Urban Farming Sustainability
description: What are the most effective strategies for implementing sustainable urban farming practices in high‑density cities?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive guidance on techniques, technologies, and policy frameworks that enable efficient, environmentally friendly urban agriculture to support food security, reduce carbon footprints, and promote community resilience. Relevant documents will include case studies, policy analyses, and technical reports on urban farming methods, especially those addressing resource constraints in dense urban environments.

title: Ethical AI in Healthcare
description: What are the key ethical considerations and best practices for implementing AI diagnostic tools in healthcare settings?
narrative: The requestor is a medical professional who wants to ensure that AI deployments comply with patient privacy, bias mitigation, regulatory standards, and governance models; relevant documents should provide comprehensive guidance, empirical evidence, and real-world case studies on ethical frameworks for AI in healthcare.

title: Solar Microgrid Storage
description: What are the most effective and cost‑efficient technologies for storing solar power in small community microgrids?
narrative: The user seeks recent academic and policy research that evaluates battery‑based, thermal, and hybrid storage solutions for microgrids in rural or island settings. Relevant documents should provide technical specifications, performance metrics, cost‑benefit analyses, and examples of successful deployments or pilot projects, as well as any regulatory or incentive frameworks that influence deployment decisions.

title: Satellite Debris Mitigation
description: Which international treaties and technical measures implemented after 2010 are most effective in reducing space debris generation?
narrative: The user is researching recent efforts to curb space debris and seeks evidence of effectiveness—studies, reports, and case analyses that quantify debris generation rates, illustrate compliance with treaties, and evaluate the impact of technical solutions (e.g., passive drag augmentation, in-orbit servicing). Relevance is determined by documents published post‑2010, focusing on space debris, addressing mitigation strategies, and providing measurable outcomes.

title: Sustainable Roofing Alternatives
description: What are the most energy‑efficient and environmentally friendly roofing materials for residential buildings in temperate climates?
narrative: The user is a homeowner seeking long‑term cost savings and a reduced carbon footprint. Relevant documents should contain comparative durability, lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions, thermal performance data, and real‑world case studies within similar temperate regions.
